# UC-13.6.2 · Grafana Data Source Query Error Rate by Type

## Description

Prometheus, InfluxDB, CloudWatch, and SQL backends fail for different reasons; error rate split by datasource_type prioritizes the right engineering owner and reveals regional or credential outages affecting one backend only.

## Value

Shortens MTTR for blank panels and prevents silent data gaps during provider incidents.

## Implementation

Emit one event per health check or sampled query from recording rules. Map `datasource_type` from Grafana’s `type` field. Join to a dashboard inventory lookup for customer impact narrative.

## SPL

```spl
(index=grafana OR index=observability) sourcetype="grafana:datasource"
| eval err=if(status!="success" OR isnotnull(error_message) OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)query error|execution error|bad gateway|timeout"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as attempts, sum(err) as errors by _time, datasource_type, datasource_name
| eval err_rate=round(100*errors/nullif(attempts,0),2)
| where err_rate > 2 AND attempts >= 20
| sort -err_rate
```

## Visualization

Stacked area (errors by type), bar chart (err_rate by datasource_name), single-value (open error budget).

## References

- [Grafana — Data sources](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/datasources/)
